 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1fpf","title":"E2E Tests: Public Profiles & Reputation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-ck7.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for public user profiles and reputation.

## Test Scenarios

### Public Profile Page
1. Profile page loads for any user
2. Avatar, bio, username display
3. Public prompts listed
4. Public collections shown
5. Reputation score visible
6. Join date shown

### Profile Editing
7. Edit profile button (own profile only)
8. Update avatar
9. Update bio
10. Update display name
11. Privacy settings respected

### Reputation Display
12. Total contributions shown
13. Average rating displayed
14. Popular prompts highlighted
15. Badges/achievements shown

### Following (if implemented)
16. Follow/unfollow user
17. Followers count updates
18. Following list accessible

## Files to Create
- e2e/profiles/public.spec.ts
- e2e/profiles/edit.spec.ts
- e2e/profiles/reputation.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/profile-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Public profiles render correctly
- Privacy respected
- Reputation accurately reflects

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1m9a","title":"Enhance copy button feedback with satisfying animation","description":"# Task: Enhance Copy Button Feedback

## Context
The copy button currently changes from Copy icon to Check icon when clicked. While functional, it lacks the "satisfying" micro-interaction that Stripe/Linear provide.

## Current Behavior
1. User clicks Copy button
2. Icon changes from Copy → Check (instant)
3. Toast appears
4. After 2s, icon changes back

## Proposed Enhancements

### 1. Icon Transition Animation
```typescript
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion';

// In PromptCard.tsx copy button
<Button onClick={handleCopy} ...>
  <AnimatePresence mode="wait">
    {copied ? (
      <motion.div
        key="check"
        initial={{ scale: 0, rotate: -180 }}
        animate={{ scale: 1, rotate: 0 }}
        exit={{ scale: 0, rotate: 180 }}
        transition={{ type: "spring", stiffness: 500, damping: 25 }}
      >
        <Check className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-500" />
      </motion.div>
    ) : (
      <motion.div
        key="copy"
        initial={{ scale: 0 }}
        animate={{ scale: 1 }}
        exit={{ scale: 0 }}
        transition={{ duration: 0.15 }}
      >
        <Copy className="w-4 h-4" />
      </motion.div>
    )}
  </AnimatePresence>
</Button>
```

### 2. Button Background Flash
```typescript
// Add success background flash
const [showFlash, setShowFlash] = useState(false);

const handleCopy = async () => {
  await navigator.clipboard.writeText(content);
  setCopied(true);
  setShowFlash(true);
  setTimeout(() => setShowFlash(false), 300);
  // ... rest of handler
};

<Button
  className={cn(
    "...",
    showFlash && "bg-emerald-100 dark:bg-emerald-900/30"
  )}
>
```

### 3. Ripple Effect (Optional)
```typescript
// CSS ripple on click
.copy-button {
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.copy-button::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0;
  background: radial-gradient(circle at center, 
    oklch(0.9 0.1 145) 0%, 
    transparent 70%
  );
  opacity: 0;
  transform: scale(0);
  transition: transform 0.3s, opacity 0.3s;
}

.copy-button.copied::after {
  opacity: 1;
  transform: scale(2);
}
```

### 4. Haptic Feedback (Mobile)
```typescript
const handleCopy = async () => {
  // Haptic feedback if available
  if ('vibrate' in navigator) {
    navigator.vibrate(50); // 50ms vibration
  }
  // ... rest of copy logic
};
```

## Design Rationale
- Spring animation on Check: Feels "bouncy" and satisfying
- Rotate effect: Draws eye, makes change obvious
- Background flash: Confirms action without being distracting
- Haptic feedback: Mobile users feel the action

## Reduced Motion Support
```typescript
const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();

// Skip animation, just swap icons
{copied ? (
  <Check className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-500" />
) : (
  <Copy className="w-4 h-4" />
)}
```

## Testing
1. Click copy - see icon spin/bounce into check
2. Background flashes briefly green
3. After 2s, icon spins back to copy
4. Mobile: feel haptic feedback
5. Reduced motion: instant icon swap
6. Multiple rapid clicks: animation doesn't break

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/PromptCard.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/PromptDetailModal.tsx (same pattern)
- apps/web/src/components/mobile/SwipeablePromptCard.tsx

## Dependencies
- Framer Motion (already available)
- Lower priority than functional improvements","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:09:39.419096088-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:09:39.419096088-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1m9a","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f3zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:24:56.158090338-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1qt5","title":"EPIC: Sharing & Collaboration","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-oam.

## Overview
Sharing enables users to distribute their prompts, packs, and collections with others - either publicly via the Swap Meet or privately via direct links.

## Sharing Modes

### 1. Public Sharing (Swap Meet)
- Make content visible to all premium users
- Appears in community browse/search
- Receives ratings and usage stats
- Builds author reputation

### 2. Private Link Sharing
- Generate unique shareable URL
- Anyone with link can view (no auth required for viewing)
- Track link views/uses
- Revoke link anytime
- Optional: password protection
- Optional: expiration date

### 3. Team/Workspace Sharing (V2)
- Share with specific team members
- Workspace-level shared library
- Role-based permissions (view/edit/admin)
- Activity feed for shared content

## Key Features

### Share Dialog
- Choose sharing mode
- Generate/copy share link
- Set link options (password, expiry)
- Preview shared view
- Social share buttons (Twitter, LinkedIn)

### Manage Shared Content
- My Shared Items dashboard
- View share statistics
- Edit sharing settings
- Revoke access
- See who accessed (for team shares)

### Receive Shared Content
- View shared content via link
- Save to own library (if premium)
- Fork/duplicate to own account
- Rate and review (if public)

### Share Targets
- Individual prompts
- Collections
- Prompt packs
- Skills

## Database Schema
- share_links (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, link_code, password_hash, expires_at, created_at)
- share_link_views (link_id, viewer_id, viewed_at, ip_hash)
- team_shares (id, content_type, content_id, team_id, permission_level, shared_by, shared_at)

## Security Considerations
- Rate limit link generation
- Obfuscated link codes (not sequential)
- Optional password protection
- View logging for audit
- DMCA/abuse reporting for public shares

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ezv2","title":"Implement changelog and what's new feature","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-xj4e.

## Task
Implement a changelog to communicate new features and updates to users.

## Why This Matters
- Users know about new features
- Reduces "I didn't know you could do that"
- Shows product is actively developed
- Marketing opportunity

## Components

### 1. Changelog Page
- /changelog - Public page
- Chronological list of updates
- Categories: New, Improved, Fixed
- Date for each entry
- SEO-friendly

### 2. What's New Modal
- Shown to users after update
- Highlights major changes
- Dismissible (remember dismissal)
- Link to full changelog

### 3. Update Badge
- Small badge on nav item
- "1 new update" indicator
- Clears when viewed

### 4. Email Digest (optional)
- Monthly changelog email
- Major updates only
- For engaged users

## Changelog Entry Format
```markdown
## March 2026

### New
- **Collections Sharing**: Share entire collections with a link
- **Dark Mode**: Toggle between light and dark themes

### Improved
- Search is now 2x faster
- Better mobile navigation

### Fixed
- Fixed issue with export failing for large collections
- Corrected billing date display
```

## Database
```typescript
export const changelogEntries = pgTable("changelog_entries", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  version: text("version"), // optional semver
  title: text("title").notNull(),
  content: text("content").notNull(), // markdown
  category: text("category").notNull(), // new, improved, fixed
  publishedAt: timestamp("published_at"),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});

// Track which users have seen which entries
export const changelogViews = pgTable("changelog_views", {
  userId: uuid("user_id").references(() => userProfiles.id),
  lastSeenEntryId: uuid("last_seen_entry_id"),
  seenAt: timestamp("seen_at").defaultNow(),
});
```

## Files to Create
- src/app/(public)/changelog/page.tsx
- src/components/changelog/whats-new-modal.tsx
- src/components/changelog/update-badge.tsx
- content/changelog/*.mdx
- src/app/api/changelog/route.ts

## Success Criteria
- Changelog updated regularly
- Users notified of changes
- Easy to maintain
- SEO-friendly

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f296","title":"Implement cookie consent and GDPR banner","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-6754. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Implement cookie consent banner for GDPR/CCPA compliance.

## Why This Matters
- Legal requirement in EU (GDPR)
- Legal requirement in California (CCPA)
- User privacy expectations
- Avoid potential fines

## Requirements

### Cookie Categories
1. **Essential** (always on)
   - Authentication
   - Security
   - Basic functionality

2. **Analytics** (optional)
   - Google Analytics
   - Usage tracking
   - Performance monitoring

3. **Marketing** (optional)
   - Advertising
   - Retargeting
   - Social media pixels

### Consent Banner
- Shown on first visit
- Clear explanation of cookies
- Accept All / Customize / Reject All
- Link to cookie policy
- Remember preference

### Cookie Settings
- Accessible from footer
- Per-category toggles
- Save preferences
- Withdraw consent anytime

### Implementation
- Don't load tracking scripts until consent
- Store preference in cookie/localStorage
- Respect Do Not Track header
- Log consent for compliance

## Technical Implementation
```typescript
// Cookie consent state
interface CookieConsent {
  essential: true; // always true
  analytics: boolean;
  marketing: boolean;
  consentedAt: string;
  version: string; // consent policy version
}
```

### Conditional Script Loading
```tsx
// Only load GA if analytics consent given
{cookieConsent.analytics && (
  <Script src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js" />
)}
```

## Files to Create
- src/components/consent/cookie-banner.tsx
- src/components/consent/cookie-settings.tsx
- src/lib/consent/cookie-consent.ts
- src/hooks/use-cookie-consent.ts
- src/app/(public)/cookies/page.tsx (Cookie Policy)

## Success Criteria
- Banner shows on first visit
- Consent respected
- Scripts load conditionally
- Settings accessible
- Preference persists

## Privacy Additions
- Respect Global Privacy Control (GPC) and DNT headers by defaulting analytics/marketing to off.
- Persist consent server-side (optional) for compliance audits; store consentVersion + timestamp.
- Gate analytics event queue until consent granted; flush only after opt-in.
- Show banner only where required (geo/IP or locale-based), but always allow manual settings link.

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f3zc","title":"Visual Polish: Loading states, animations, and micro-interactions","description":"# Epic: Visual Polish & Micro-interactions

## Background & Problem Statement

Stripe-level polish comes from attention to details that most users can't articulate but definitely feel. Our current implementation is functionally complete but lacks the subtle animations and feedback that make interfaces feel "premium."

### Missing Polish Elements

#### 1. Loading States
- **SpotlightSearch**: Shows "Improving..." text during semantic rerank, but no skeleton or visual feedback for the actual results
- **Filter changes**: Results snap instantly (no transition)
- **Basket operations**: Export button becomes disabled but no progress indicator

#### 2. Content Transitions
- When filtering, results appear/disappear instantly
- No fade transitions between states
- Modal open/close is smooth, but content inside isn't staggered

#### 3. Micro-interactions
- Basket items have no entrance animation when added
- Hover states on some elements are too subtle
- Copy button changes icon but could have more satisfying feedback
- No celebration moments (e.g., when all prompts copied)

#### 4. Visual Feedback Gaps
- No skeleton loading for SpotlightSearch during rerank
- Filter count doesn't animate when changing
- Basket badge count doesn't animate

## Stripe/Linear Examples to Emulate
1. **Skeleton loading**: Shimmer effect on placeholders
2. **Staggered entrances**: Cards animate in with slight delay
3. **State transitions**: Smooth crossfade between loading/loaded/empty
4. **Hover micro-animations**: Subtle scale or shadow changes
5. **Action feedback**: Button press effects, checkmark animations

## Success Criteria
- [ ] SpotlightSearch shows skeleton during rerank
- [ ] Filter changes have subtle fade transition
- [ ] Basket items animate on add/remove
- [ ] Copy success has satisfying visual feedback
- [ ] All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion

## Design Principles
- Subtle > Flashy (animations should enhance, not distract)
- Fast > Slow (keep durations under 300ms for most)
- Purposeful (every animation should communicate state)
- Accessible (reduced motion support)

## Dependencies
- Should be done AFTER color consistency and UX improvements
- These are enhancements, not critical fixes

## Estimated Scope
- 5-6 files to modify
- Medium complexity (Framer Motion knowledge required)
- Lower priority than functional UX fixes","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:40:28.280178744-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T10:07:02.032893865-05:00"}

### Missing Polish Elements

#### 1. Loading States
- **SpotlightSearch**: Shows "Improving..." text during semantic rerank, but no skeleton or visual feedback for the actual results
- **Filter changes**: Results snap instantly (no transition)
- **Basket operations**: Export button becomes disabled but no progress indicator

#### 2. Content Transitions
- When filtering, results appear/disappear instantly
- No fade transitions between states
- Modal open/close is smooth, but content inside isn't staggered

#### 3. Micro-interactions
- Basket items have no entrance animation when added
- Hover states on some elements are too subtle
- Copy button changes icon but could have more satisfying feedback
- No celebration moments (e.g., when all prompts copied)

#### 4. Visual Feedback Gaps
- No skeleton loading for SpotlightSearch during rerank
- Filter count doesn't animate when changing
- Basket badge count doesn't animate

## Stripe/Linear Examples to Emulate
1. **Skeleton loading**: Shimmer effect on placeholders
2. **Staggered entrances**: Cards animate in with slight delay
3. **State transitions**: Smooth crossfade between loading/loaded/empty
4. **Hover micro-animations**: Subtle scale or shadow changes
5. **Action feedback**: Button press effects, checkmark animations

## Success Criteria
- [ ] SpotlightSearch shows skeleton during rerank
- [ ] Filter changes have subtle fade transition
- [ ] Basket items animate on add/remove
- [ ] Copy success has satisfying visual feedback
- [ ] All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion

## Design Principles
- Subtle > Flashy (animations should enhance, not distract)
- Fast > Slow (keep durations under 300ms for most)
- Purposeful (every animation should communicate state)
- Accessible (reduced motion support)

## Dependencies
- Should be done AFTER color consistency and UX improvements
- These are enhancements, not critical fixes

## Estimated Scope
- 5-6 files to modify
- Medium complexity (Framer Motion knowledge required)
- Lower priority than functional UX fixes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:40:28.280178744-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T10:16:50.563316003-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T10:16:50.563316003-05:00","close_reason":"Epic complete: copy button animations, basket item animations, and accessibility (prefers-reduced-motion) all implemented"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f7wf","title":"Add fade transitions when filter results change","description":"# Task: Add Fade Transitions for Filter Changes

## Context
When users apply or remove filters, the PromptGrid results snap instantly. This feels abrupt compared to the smooth card entrance animations. Stripe/Linear use subtle crossfades for content changes.

## Current Behavior
1. User clicks category filter
2. Results instantly swap (jarring)
3. New cards animate in (but old ones just disappear)

## Proposed Behavior
1. User clicks category filter
2. Current results fade out slightly (100ms)
3. New results fade in with staggered animation
4. Smooth transition feels intentional

## Implementation

### Option A: Layout Animation (Recommended)
Use Framer Motion's layout animation on the grid:

```tsx
// PromptGrid.tsx
<motion.div 
  className="grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4"
  layout
>
  <AnimatePresence mode="popLayout">
    {prompts.map((prompt, index) => (
      <motion.div
        key={prompt.id}
        layout
        initial={{ opacity: 0, scale: 0.95 }}
        animate={{ opacity: 1, scale: 1 }}
        exit={{ opacity: 0, scale: 0.95 }}
        transition={{ 
          duration: 0.2,
          delay: Math.min(index * 0.03, 0.15) // Cap delay at 150ms
        }}
      >
        <PromptCard prompt={prompt} index={index} ... />
      </motion.div>
    ))}
  </AnimatePresence>
</motion.div>
```

### Option B: Simple Fade Wrapper
Wrap the entire grid in a fade container that triggers on filter change:

```tsx
// page.tsx
const [isTransitioning, setIsTransitioning] = useState(false);
const prevFilters = useRef(filters);

useEffect(() => {
  if (JSON.stringify(prevFilters.current) !== JSON.stringify(filters)) {
    setIsTransitioning(true);
    setTimeout(() => setIsTransitioning(false), 100);
    prevFilters.current = filters;
  }
}, [filters]);

<motion.div
  animate={{ opacity: isTransitioning ? 0.5 : 1 }}
  transition={{ duration: 0.1 }}
>
  <PromptGrid prompts={filteredPrompts} ... />
</motion.div>
```

### Option C: CSS-Only Transition
Add CSS transition to the grid container:

```css
.prompt-grid {
  transition: opacity 0.15s ease-out;
}

.prompt-grid.transitioning {
  opacity: 0.5;
}
```

## Recommended Approach
**Option A** is best for Stripe-level polish because:
- Individual card animations look premium
- Layout animation handles position changes
- AnimatePresence properly exits old cards
- Staggered delay creates cascade effect

## Design Rationale
- Transitions acknowledge user action
- Fade suggests "content is changing"
- Stagger creates sense of order
- Fast durations (< 200ms) feel responsive

## Testing
1. Apply category filter - cards fade smoothly
2. Remove filter - cards fade back
3. Add tag filter - subset fades in
4. Clear all filters - full set reappears smoothly
5. Rapid filter changes - no janky behavior
6. Reduced motion - instant (no animation)

## Performance Considerations
- layout animations can be expensive with many items
- Cap stagger delay to prevent long waits
- Use will-change: transform on cards if needed
- Test on lower-end devices

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/PromptGrid.tsx
- Possibly apps/web/src/app/page.tsx

## Dependencies
- Framer Motion (already included)
- PromptCard already has entrance animation (coordinate)
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:41:26.805462194-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:41:26.805462194-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f7wf","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:43:35.79058631-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fbcl","title":"EPIC: Landing Page & SEO","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-5cy.

## Overview
Marketing landing page and SEO optimization for organic discovery.

## Landing Page (/)

### Hero Section
- Headline: Value proposition
- Subheadline: Brief description
- CTA buttons: "Get Started Free" / "View Pricing"
- Hero image/animation showing product

### Features Section
- Grid of key features with icons
- Swap Meet / Community
- Prompt Packs
- Skills
- Collections
- CLI Integration
- Analytics

### How It Works
- Step-by-step visual guide
- 1. Sign up with Google
- 2. Browse or create prompts
- 3. Organize and share

### Social Proof
- Testimonials (when we have them)
- User count / prompt count stats
- "As seen in" logos (if applicable)

### Pricing Preview
- Simple free vs premium comparison
- Link to full /pricing page

### Footer CTA
- Final call to action
- Email signup for updates (optional)

## SEO Optimization

### Meta Tags
- Dynamic title and description per page
- Open Graph tags for social sharing
- Twitter Card tags
- Canonical URLs

### Dynamic OG Images
- Generate OG images for shared prompts
- Include title, author, rating
- Branded template

### Technical SEO
- Sitemap.xml generation
- robots.txt configuration
- Schema.org structured data
- Fast page loads (Core Web Vitals)

## Pages to Create
- / (landing)
- /pricing (detailed pricing)
- /about (optional)
- /changelog (what's new)

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fhci","title":"Implement admin role permissions system","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-wbx0. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create granular role-based access control for admin functions.

## Roles
- Super Admin: Full access, can manage other admins
- Admin: Full content and user management
- Moderator: Content review, user warnings
- Support: Read-only, can escalate

## Permissions
```typescript
const PERMISSIONS = {
  // User management
  'users.view': ['moderator', 'support', 'admin', 'super_admin'],
  'users.edit': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  'users.suspend': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  'users.delete': ['super_admin'],
  
  // Content moderation
  'content.view_reported': ['moderator', 'support', 'admin', 'super_admin'],
  'content.moderate': ['moderator', 'admin', 'super_admin'],
  'content.delete': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  
  // Billing
  'billing.view': ['support', 'admin', 'super_admin'],
  'billing.refund': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  
  // Admin management
  'admins.view': ['admin', 'super_admin'],
  'admins.manage': ['super_admin'],
};
```

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const adminRoles = pgTable('admin_roles', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id),
  role: text('role', { enum: ['super_admin', 'admin', 'moderator', 'support'] }),
  grantedBy: uuid('granted_by').references(() => userProfiles.id),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
});
```

## UI
- Role indicator in admin header
- Permission-gated UI elements
- Role management page (super admin)

## Success Criteria
- Proper permission enforcement
- Clear role boundaries
- Audit log for role changes
- API and UI consistent","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:24:06.423006677-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T21:42:30.342152907-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T21:42:30.342152907-05:00","close_reason":"Admin role permissions system implemented in apps/web/src/lib/admin/permissions.ts: AdminRole types (super_admin/admin/moderator/support), PERMISSIONS matrix, checkAdminPermission function with token-based auth.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fhci","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-kk7l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:36.197636282-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fjw1","title":"Add basket item add/remove animations","description":"# Task: Animate Basket Item Add/Remove

## Context
When items are added to or removed from the basket, they appear/disappear instantly. This feels abrupt compared to the smooth card animations elsewhere. Stripe/Linear use subtle animations for list changes.

## Current Behavior
- Add item: Appears instantly at end of list
- Remove item: Disappears instantly
- Count badge: Updates without animation

## Proposed Solution

### 1. List Item Animation (BasketSidebar.tsx)
```typescript
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion';

// Wrap basket items in AnimatePresence
<div className="space-y-2">
  <AnimatePresence mode="popLayout">
    {basketItems.map((item, index) => (
      <motion.div
        key={item.id}
        layout
        initial={{ opacity: 0, x: -20, height: 0 }}
        animate={{ opacity: 1, x: 0, height: 'auto' }}
        exit={{ opacity: 0, x: 20, height: 0 }}
        transition={{ 
          duration: 0.2,
          delay: index * 0.03, // Stagger for multiple adds
          ease: [0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1]
        }}
      >
        <BasketItemRow item={item} ... />
      </motion.div>
    ))}
  </AnimatePresence>
</div>
```

### 2. Badge Count Animation (Nav.tsx or BasketSidebar.tsx)
```typescript
// Animated counter for basket badge
const [displayCount, setDisplayCount] = useState(count);
const springCount = useSpring(count, { stiffness: 300, damping: 30 });

useEffect(() => {
  return springCount.onChange(latest => {
    setDisplayCount(Math.round(latest));
  });
}, [springCount]);

// Or simpler: scale animation on change
<motion.span
  key={count} // Remount on change triggers animation
  initial={{ scale: 1.5, opacity: 0 }}
  animate={{ scale: 1, opacity: 1 }}
  transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
  className="..."
>
  {count}
</motion.span>
```

### 3. Add Success Feedback
```typescript
// Flash effect when item added
const [justAdded, setJustAdded] = useState<string | null>(null);

const handleAdd = (id: string) => {
  addToBasket(id);
  setJustAdded(id);
  setTimeout(() => setJustAdded(null), 1000);
};

// In list:
<motion.div
  className={cn(
    "...",
    justAdded === item.id && "ring-2 ring-emerald-500"
  )}
  animate={justAdded === item.id ? { 
    backgroundColor: ['var(--emerald-50)', 'transparent'] 
  } : {}}
>
```

## Design Rationale
- Slide in from left: Suggests item came from the content area
- Slide out to right: Suggests item is being dismissed
- Layout animation: Other items smoothly reposition
- Stagger delay: Multiple adds feel organized, not chaotic
- Badge pulse: Draws attention to count change

## Reduced Motion Support
```typescript
const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();

const itemVariants = prefersReducedMotion 
  ? { initial: {}, animate: {}, exit: {} }
  : { initial: { opacity: 0, x: -20 }, ... };
```

## Testing
1. Add item - slides in from left with fade
2. Remove item - slides out to right with fade
3. Add multiple - staggered entrance
4. Badge count - subtle pulse on change
5. Reduced motion - instant changes, no animation
6. Empty state transition - smooth crossfade

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/Nav.tsx (badge animation)

## Dependencies
- Framer Motion (already available)
- Should be done after color consistency fixes","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:09:33.777021051-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:09:33.777021051-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fjw1","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f3zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:24:51.005541395-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fo8i","title":"Implement moderation appeal process","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-u8t6.

## Task
Allow users to appeal moderation decisions.

## Why This Matters
- Fairness to users
- Catch false positives
- Build trust
- Legal protection

## Appeal Process

### 1. Submit Appeal
- From suspension/removal notice
- Explain why decision wrong
- Provide evidence
- One appeal per action

### 2. Review Queue
- Admin reviews appeals
- See original violation
- See user explanation
- Make decision

### 3. Outcomes
- Upheld (keep action)
- Overturned (reverse action)
- Modified (reduce severity)

### 4. Communication
- Notify user of outcome
- Explain reasoning
- Final decision (no re-appeal)

## Database
```typescript
export const moderationAppeals = pgTable('moderation_appeals', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  
  // What is being appealed
  moderationActionId: uuid('moderation_action_id').notNull(),
  actionType: text('action_type').notNull(), // content_removed, account_suspended, etc.
  
  // Appellant
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  
  // Appeal content
  reason: text('reason').notNull(),
  evidence: text('evidence'),
  
  // Status
  status: text('status').default('pending'), // pending, reviewing, upheld, overturned, modified
  
  // Review
  reviewedBy: uuid('reviewed_by').references(() => userProfiles.id),
  reviewedAt: timestamp('reviewed_at'),
  reviewNotes: text('review_notes'),
  outcome: text('outcome'),
  
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});
```

## UI Components
- AppealForm - Submit appeal
- AppealStatus - Track appeal status
- AppealQueue - Admin queue
- AppealReview - Admin review panel

## API Endpoints
- POST /api/appeals - Submit appeal
- GET /api/appeals/[id] - Get appeal status
- GET /api/admin/appeals - List appeals (admin)
- PATCH /api/admin/appeals/[id] - Resolve appeal (admin)

## Limits
- 1 appeal per moderation action
- 7 days to submit appeal
- Admin has 14 days to review
- No re-appeals after decision

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/appeals.ts
- src/app/api/appeals/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/appeals/[id]/page.tsx
- src/app/(app)/admin/appeals/page.tsx
- src/components/moderation/appeal-form.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Appeals can be submitted
- Admin can review efficiently
- Users notified of outcomes
- Process is fair and documented

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fuc1","title":"UX Flow Improvements: Filter discoverability and clarity","description":"# Epic: UX Flow Improvements

## Background & Problem Statement

User testing and UX audit revealed several friction points in the main browsing experience:

### Issue 1: "Clear All Filters" Discoverability
**Current state:** Plain text link that only appears when filters are active
**Problem:** 
- Users don't notice it (blends with surrounding text)
- No visual affordance before filters are applied
- Uses indigo accent inconsistent with design system

### Issue 2: Featured Section Confusion
**Current state:** Featured prompts appear before filter controls
**Problem:**
- Filters don't affect featured section (confusing)
- No clear visual boundary between "featured" and "browse" modes
- Users may think filtering is broken

### Issue 3: Basket Empty State
**Current state:** Basic icon + text ("Your basket is empty")
**Problem:**
- Not engaging or helpful
- No CTA to guide users
- Missed opportunity for onboarding

### Issue 4: Active Filter Visibility
**Current state:** Only text description below results header
**Problem:**
- Easy to miss that filters are active
- No visual chips/badges for individual filters
- Can't remove individual tags without going back to filter section

## Success Criteria
- [ ] "Clear filters" is a visible button (ghost variant with X icon)
- [ ] Featured section has clear visual separation
- [ ] Basket empty state includes helpful CTA
- [ ] Active filters shown as removable chips
- [ ] All changes maintain Stripe/Linear aesthetic

## Design Principles
- Make hidden things visible
- Reduce cognitive load
- Provide clear feedback
- Guide users toward actions

## Dependencies
- Should be done AFTER color consistency (Phase 1)
- These are additive features, not breaking changes

## Estimated Scope
- 3-4 files to modify
- Medium complexity (some new components)
